gaps.snippet: impl CertMode { # [doc = " All three modes, weakest-to-strongest certification depth — for exhaustive iteration in tests"] # [doc = " and tooling."] pub const ALL : [CertMode ; 3] = [CertMode :: Fast , CertMode :: Balanced , CertMode :: Certified] ; # [doc = " Certification **depth**, `0` = [`Fast`](CertMode::Fast) (least) … `2` ="] # [doc = " [`Certified`](CertMode::Certified) (most). Higher = more certification machinery engaged."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This orders the modes; it is **not** a guarantee strength — a stronger mode never upgrades a"] # [doc = " value's [`GuaranteeStrength`](crate::guarantee::GuaranteeStrength) (VR-5). Composing across"] # [doc = " modes is an explicit, visible event (RFC-0034 §3.1), never a silent upgrade."] # [must_use] pub fn depth (self) -> u8 { match self { CertMode :: Fast => 0 , CertMode :: Balanced => 1 , CertMode :: Certified => 2 , } } # [doc = " Gate an operation's *intended* intrinsic guarantee strength by this mode (RFC-0034 §7; M-787)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `Fast` does not run the trials behind [`Empirical`](GuaranteeStrength::Empirical) or the proof"] # [doc = " behind [`Proven`](GuaranteeStrength::Proven), so it **floors both to**"] # [doc = " [`Declared`](GuaranteeStrength::Declared) — the honest \"computed, but its bound is asserted not"] # [doc = " verified in this mode\" tag (VR-5: never claim a strength you did not establish). The free,"] # [doc = " **structural** [`Exact`](GuaranteeStrength::Exact) (e.g. a bijective binary↔ternary swap) passes"] # [doc = " untouched, and an already-`Declared` tag is unchanged. `Balanced` and `Certified` run the"] # [doc = " machinery, so they pass every strength through identically (the mechanism is unchanged)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the policy primitive; the operation layer applies it when it constructs a result's"] # [doc = " `Meta` (the bound's basis is relabelled to `UserDeclared` in lockstep so M-I1…M-I4 stay"] # [doc = " consistent — wired where operations become mode-aware, M-788 onward). It guarantees the M-787"] # [doc = " invariant directly: **no `Fast` result ever carries `Empirical`/`Proven`.**"] # [must_use] pub fn gate_guarantee (self , intended : GuaranteeStrength) -> GuaranteeStrength { use GuaranteeStrength :: { Declared , Empirical , Exact , Proven } ; match self { CertMode :: Fast => match intended { Exact => Exact , Proven | Empirical | Declared => Declared , } , CertMode :: Balanced | CertMode :: Certified => intended , } } # [doc = " Gate an operation's *intended* `(guarantee, bound)` **pair** by this mode, reconciling the"] # [doc = " bound's basis with the floored guarantee so the result still satisfies the `Meta` invariants"] # [doc = " **M-I1…M-I4** (RFC-0034 §7; M-788 — the bound/basis half [`gate_guarantee`] explicitly"] # [doc = " deferred)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The guarantee is gated exactly as [`gate_guarantee`](CertMode::gate_guarantee). The subtlety"] # [doc = " is the *bound*: when `Fast` floors a would-be [`Proven`](GuaranteeStrength::Proven)/"] # [doc = " [`Empirical`](GuaranteeStrength::Empirical) result to [`Declared`](GuaranteeStrength::Declared),"] # [doc = " the operation has *computed* a bound value (an ε/δ) but `Fast` ran **no** certification"] # [doc = " machinery to check it — so the bound's basis ([`ProvenThm`](BoundBasis::ProvenThm)/"] # [doc = " [`EmpiricalFit`](BoundBasis::EmpiricalFit)) is no longer earned. Carrying it unchanged would"] # [doc = " violate **M-I4** (`Declared ⟹ UserDeclared`). The honest reconciliation (VR-5: never claim a"] # [doc = " basis you did not establish) is to **keep the computed bound *value* but relabel its basis to"] # [doc = " [`UserDeclared`](BoundBasis::UserDeclared)** — \"computed, but asserted-not-verified in this"] # [doc = " mode\" — which is exactly the basis M-I4 requires for a `Declared` tag."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Concretely, mode by intended strength:"] # [doc = " - **`Fast` + `Exact`** → `(Exact, None)`. `Exact` is structural and bound-free; the caller's"] # [doc = "   `bound` is already `None` by M-I1 (a non-`None` bound on an `Exact` intent is itself a"] # [doc = "   bug the `Meta` constructor would reject), and this preserves M-I1."] # [doc = " - **`Fast` + `Proven`/`Empirical`/`Declared`** → `(Declared, bound.relabel(UserDeclared))`."] # [doc = "   The value is kept; only the basis is demoted, satisfying M-I4. (An already-`Declared`"] # [doc = "   intent already carries a `UserDeclared` basis, so the relabel is a no-op for it.)"] # [doc = " - **`Balanced`/`Certified`** → `(intended, bound)` unchanged — the machinery runs, so the"] # [doc = "   computed basis is earned and passes through (the mechanism is preserved)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The returned pair is **always** one the `Meta` constructor (which enforces M-I1…M-I4) accepts"] # [doc = " — that round-trip is the unit-tested contract (`gate_result` then `Meta::new` never errors on"] # [doc = " the invariant check). Numeric well-formedness of the bound is unchanged by the relabel (the"] # [doc = " payload is untouched), so a well-formed input stays well-formed."] # [must_use] pub fn gate_result (self , intended_guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , intended_bound : Option < Bound > ,) -> (GuaranteeStrength , Option < Bound >) { use GuaranteeStrength :: { Declared , Empirical , Exact , Proven } ; match self { CertMode :: Fast => match intended_guarantee { Exact => (Exact , None) , Proven | Empirical | Declared => { (Declared , intended_bound . map (relabel_user_declared)) } } , CertMode :: Balanced | CertMode :: Certified => (intended_guarantee , intended_bound) , } } }

gaps.snippet: impl Canon { # [doc = " Encode a [`CoreValue`]'s identity-bearing content (a representation value's repr+payload, or"] # [doc = " a datum's constructor+fields). `Meta` / the datum summary are dynamic and excluded."] # [doc = " Iterative for the `Data` arm (delegates to the iterative [`Canon::datum`])."] pub (crate) fn core_value (& mut self , v : & CoreValue) { match v { CoreValue :: Repr (rv) => self . value (rv) , CoreValue :: Data (d) => self . datum (d) , } } # [doc = " Encode a [`Datum`]: its constructor reference then each field (order significant), **pre-order"] # [doc = " and iterative** (RFC-0041 §4.5) — byte-for-byte identical to the former recursive encoding, so"] # [doc = " content addresses are unchanged, but with bounded native-stack use on a deep datum spine."] pub (crate) fn datum (& mut self , d : & Datum) { self . datum_header (d) ; let mut work : Vec < & CoreValue > = Vec :: new () ; for f in d . fields () . iter () . rev () { work . push (f) ; } while let Some (cv) = work . pop () { match cv { CoreValue :: Repr (rv) => self . value (rv) , CoreValue :: Data (inner) => { self . datum_header (inner) ; for f in inner . fields () . iter () . rev () { work . push (f) ; } } } } } # [doc = " Emit one datum's header bytes (tag · ctor-ref · field count) — the per-node prefix the"] # [doc = " recursive `datum` encoder wrote before recursing into fields."] fn datum_header (& mut self , d : & Datum) { self . tag (crate :: content :: tag :: DATUM) ; self . ctor_ref (d . ctor ()) ; self . u64 (d . fields () . len () as u64) ; } }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " How trustworthy a value's representation/bound is. Honesty is monotone-downward: an operation's"] # [doc = " result is never stronger than its weakest input (the [`meet`](GuaranteeStrength::meet))."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The `serde` form is the bare string `\"Exact\"|\"Proven\"|\"Empirical\"|\"Declared\"`"] # [doc = " (`guarantee.schema.json`)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq , serde :: Serialize , serde :: Deserialize)] pub enum GuaranteeStrength { # [doc = " No approximation; `bound == None` (M-I1)."] Exact , # [doc = " Approximate with a machine-checked bound (basis `ProvenThm`)."] Proven , # [doc = " Approximate with an empirically-validated bound (basis `EmpiricalFit`)."] Empirical , # [doc = " Approximate with a user-asserted, unvalidated bound (basis `UserDeclared`); always flagged."] Declared , }
gaps.reason: enum `GuaranteeStrength` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: impl GuaranteeStrength { # [doc = " The strongest strength — the identity of [`meet`](Self::meet) and the unit of"] # [doc = " [`meet_all`](Self::meet_all): `meet(Exact, g) == g` for all `g`."] pub const TOP : GuaranteeStrength = GuaranteeStrength :: Exact ; # [doc = " All four strengths, strongest-to-weakest — for exhaustive iteration in tests and tooling."] pub const ALL : [GuaranteeStrength ; 4] = [GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared ,] ; # [doc = " Lattice rank, `0` = strongest (`Exact`) … `3` = weakest (`Declared`). The [`meet`](Self::meet)"] # [doc = " of two strengths is the one with the larger rank (the weakest)."] # [must_use] pub fn rank (self) -> u8 { match self { GuaranteeStrength :: Exact => 0 , GuaranteeStrength :: Proven => 1 , GuaranteeStrength :: Empirical => 2 , GuaranteeStrength :: Declared => 3 , } } # [doc = " The lattice **meet** (greatest lower bound): the *weakest* of `self` and `other`"] # [doc = " (RFC-0001 §3.4/§4.7). Honesty is monotone-downward — a result can never be stronger than"] # [doc = " its weakest input — so composition takes the meet. The meet is the strength with the"] # [doc = " larger [`rank`](Self::rank); on a tie the operands are equal, so either is returned."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Algebraically a meet-semilattice: **commutative**, **associative**, **idempotent**, with"] # [doc = " identity [`TOP`](Self::TOP) (`Exact`). These laws are verified by *exhaustion* over all"] # [doc = " 4×4(×4) tuples — the value space is finite, so this is a complete check, not sampling."] # [must_use] pub fn meet (self , other : GuaranteeStrength) -> GuaranteeStrength { if self . rank () >= other . rank () { self } else { other } } # [doc = " Propagate guarantees through an operation (RFC-0001 §4.7):"] # [doc = " `guarantee(result) = meet(guarantee(v_1), …, guarantee(v_n), g_f)`, where `g_f` is the"] # [doc = " operation's own intrinsic strength. Folds [`meet`](Self::meet) over the inputs starting"] # [doc = " from `intrinsic`. With no inputs the result is `intrinsic` itself."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This is the *only* sanctioned way to derive an operation's result strength — disclosure can"] # [doc = " only degrade, never spuriously upgrade (VR-3/VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn propagate (intrinsic : GuaranteeStrength , inputs : impl IntoIterator < Item = GuaranteeStrength > ,) -> GuaranteeStrength { inputs . into_iter () . fold (intrinsic , GuaranteeStrength :: meet) } # [doc = " The meet of a sequence of strengths, weakest-wins, starting from [`TOP`](Self::TOP)"] # [doc = " (`Exact`). An empty sequence yields `Exact` (the identity). Equivalent to"] # [doc = " [`propagate`](Self::propagate) with an `Exact` intrinsic."] # [must_use] pub fn meet_all (strengths : impl IntoIterator < Item = GuaranteeStrength >) -> GuaranteeStrength { Self :: propagate (GuaranteeStrength :: TOP , strengths) } }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: GuaranteeStrength ; use super :: GuaranteeStrength :: { Declared , Empirical , Exact , Proven } ; # [test] fn ranks_are_strongest_to_weakest () { assert ! (Exact . rank () < Proven . rank ()) ; assert ! (Proven . rank () < Empirical . rank ()) ; assert ! (Empirical . rank () < Declared . rank ()) ; } # [doc = " The meet equals the weakest (larger-rank) operand — for every one of the 16 pairs."] # [test] fn meet_is_weakest_for_all_pairs () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for b in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { let m = a . meet (b) ; assert_eq ! (m . rank () , a . rank () . max (b . rank ()) , "meet({a:?},{b:?})") ; assert ! (m == a || m == b) ; } } } # [doc = " **Commutativity**, exhaustively over all 4×4 pairs."] # [test] fn meet_is_commutative () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for b in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (a . meet (b) , b . meet (a) , "commutativity {a:?},{b:?}") ; } } } # [doc = " **Idempotence**, all 4."] # [test] fn meet_is_idempotent () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (a . meet (a) , a , "idempotence {a:?}") ; } } # [doc = " **Associativity**, exhaustively over all 4×4×4 triples."] # [test] fn meet_is_associative () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for b in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { for c in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (a . meet (b) . meet (c) , a . meet (b . meet (c)) , "associativity {a:?},{b:?},{c:?}") ; } } } } # [doc = " **Identity**: `Exact` (`TOP`) is the unit on both sides, for every strength."] # [test] fn exact_is_the_identity () { assert_eq ! (GuaranteeStrength :: TOP , Exact) ; for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (Exact . meet (a) , a , "left identity {a:?}") ; assert_eq ! (a . meet (Exact) , a , "right identity {a:?}") ; } } # [doc = " `Declared` is the bottom: it absorbs everything (the weakest is always weakest)."] # [test] fn declared_is_absorbing () { for a in GuaranteeStrength :: ALL { assert_eq ! (Declared . meet (a) , Declared , "{a:?}") ; assert_eq ! (a . meet (Declared) , Declared , "{a:?}") ; } } # [doc = " `propagate` realizes RFC-0001 §4.7: the result is the meet of all inputs and the intrinsic"] # [doc = " strength — an `Empirical` input drags an otherwise-`Exact` op down to `Empirical`."] # [test] fn propagate_takes_the_meet_of_inputs_and_intrinsic () { assert_eq ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A content address, e.g. `blake3:af13…` (64 hex). The kernel hash is **BLAKE3** (fixed in M-103),"] # [doc = " rendered as `blake3:<64-hex>`; this type fixes the shape (`<algo>:<digest>`, matching the"] # [doc = " `provenance.schema.json` pattern) and stays algorithm-agnostic so a future migration is a value"] # [doc = " change, not a type change."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Two validation strengths are offered, deliberately distinct (DN-40 wave-2):"] # [doc = " - [`ContentHash::parse`] / [`ContentHash::from_parts`] — **shape-only**, exactly matching the"] # [doc = "   normative JSON-Schema pattern `^[a-z0-9]+:[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$` (which documents that it \"fixes only"] # [doc = "   the shape\"). This is what (de)serialization uses, so the Rust type and the schema agree."] # [doc = " - [`ContentHash::parse_digest`] — **algorithm-aware**: additionally requires that a `blake3`"] # [doc = "   address carry a *real* 64-lowercase-hex digest, so a shape-valid-but-bogus stub like"] # [doc = "   `\"blake3:abc\"` is rejected (G2). This is the **centralized** check consumers that hold a"] # [doc = "   genuine, identity-bearing pin (e.g. a manifest dependency hash — DN-40 A3) should call, instead"] # [doc = "   of each re-deriving the 64-hex rule. See [`ContentHash::digest_well_formed`]."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq , Hash , PartialOrd , Ord)] pub struct ContentHash (String) ;
gaps.reason: struct `ContentHash` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: impl ContentHash { # [doc = " Parse a content address, validating its **shape only**: `algo` is `[a-z0-9]+`, `digest` is"] # [doc = " `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`, separated by a single `:`. This is exactly the normative schema pattern"] # [doc = " (`provenance.schema.json`: `^[a-z0-9]+:[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$`, \"fixes only the shape\"), so the Rust"] # [doc = " type and the on-wire schema agree — (de)serialization routes through here. Returns `None` if"] # [doc = " malformed."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Note:** this does *not* check that a `blake3` digest is a real 64-hex digest — a stub like"] # [doc = " `\"blake3:abc\"` is shape-valid and accepted here. A caller holding an identity-bearing pin"] # [doc = " (not a placeholder) should use [`ContentHash::parse_digest`], which adds the algorithm-aware"] # [doc = " digest check (DN-40 wave-2)."] # [must_use] pub fn parse (s : & str) -> Option < Self > { let (algo , digest) = s . split_once (':') ? ; if algo . is_empty () || digest . is_empty () { return None ; } if ! algo . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_lowercase () || b . is_ascii_digit ()) { return None ; } if ! digest . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_alphanumeric () || b == b'_' || b == b'-') { return None ; } Some (ContentHash (s . to_owned ())) } # [doc = " Parse a content address with **algorithm-aware digest validation** (DN-40 wave-2) — the"] # [doc = " centralized form. Validates the shape (as [`parse`](Self::parse)) **and**, for the kernel's"] # [doc = " fixed algorithm `blake3` (M-103), that the digest is a *real* digest: exactly"] # [doc = " [`BLAKE3_HEX_LEN`] lowercase hex `[0-9a-f]` (what `blake3::hash().to_hex()` emits). So a"] # [doc = " shape-valid-but-bogus stub like `\"blake3:abc\"` is **rejected**, not silently accepted (G2)."] # [doc = " Unknown algorithms stay **permissive** (shape-only) for forward-compatibility: a future hash"] # [doc = " migration is a value change, not a type change, and the kernel mints only `blake3` today."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " This centralizes the check DN-40 A3 first added inline in `mycelium-proj`'s manifest parser,"] # [doc = " so every consumer that holds an identity-bearing pin can share one canonical implementation"] # [doc = " rather than re-deriving the 64-hex rule. Returns `None` if malformed. **It is kept distinct"] # [doc = " from [`parse`](Self::parse)** because the normative JSON Schema is shape-only by design;"] # [doc = " (de)serialization must stay schema-aligned, so the stricter rule is opt-in at the call sites"] # [doc = " that want it."] # [must_use] pub fn parse_digest (s : & str) -> Option < Self > { let h = Self :: parse (s) ? ; if Self :: digest_well_formed (h . algo () , h . digest ()) { Some (h) } else { None } } # [doc = " Is `digest` a well-formed digest for `algo`? For `blake3` (M-103): exactly [`BLAKE3_HEX_LEN`]"] # [doc = " lowercase hex `[0-9a-f]`. For any other (forward-compat) algorithm: permissive — the address"] # [doc = " charset already enforced by [`parse`](Self::parse) is sufficient, so this returns `true`."] # [doc = " This is the single source of truth for the algorithm-aware digest rule (DN-40 wave-2)."] # [must_use] pub fn digest_well_formed (algo : & str , digest : & str) -> bool { match algo { "blake3" => { digest . len () == BLAKE3_HEX_LEN && digest . bytes () . all (| b | b . is_ascii_digit () || (b'a' ..= b'f') . contains (& b)) } _ => true , } } # [doc = " Does this address carry a well-formed digest for its own algorithm (DN-40 wave-2)? Convenience"] # [doc = " over [`digest_well_formed`](Self::digest_well_formed) for an already-parsed address — lets a"] # [doc = " consumer that received a [`ContentHash`] via the shape-only path (e.g. `Deserialize`) assert"] # [doc = " the algorithm-aware rule without re-parsing."] # [must_use] pub fn has_well_formed_digest (& self) -> bool { Self :: digest_well_formed (self . algo () , self . digest ()) } # [doc = " Build a content address from an algorithm tag and digest, validating the **shape** (`algo` is"] # [doc = " `[a-z0-9]+`, `digest` is `[A-Za-z0-9_-]+`). Returns `None` if either part is malformed. This"] # [doc = " is the constructor the content-addressing pass (M-103) uses after computing a digest. Because"] # [doc = " M-103 feeds it a real 64-hex `blake3` digest, the shape check suffices; a caller that wants"] # [doc = " the algorithm-aware guarantee on untrusted parts can compose with"] # [doc = " [`digest_well_formed`](Self::digest_well_formed) or use [`parse_digest`](Self::parse_digest)."] # [must_use] pub fn from_parts (algo : & str , digest : & str) -> Option < Self > { Self :: parse (& format ! ("{algo}:{digest}")) } # [doc = " The algorithm tag (the part before `:`), e.g. `blake3`."] # [must_use] pub fn algo (& self) -> & str { self . 0 . split_once (':') . map_or ("" , | (a , _) | a) } # [doc = " The digest (the part after `:`)."] # [must_use] pub fn digest (& self) -> & str { self . 0 . split_once (':') . map_or ("" , | (_ , d) | d) } # [doc = " The address as a string slice."] # [must_use] pub fn as_str (& self) -> & str { & self . 0 } }

(s , "{}" , render_const (v)) ; } Node :: Var (x) => { match scope . iter () . rev () . find (| (orig , _) | orig == x) { Some ((_ , canon)) => { let _ = writeln ! (s , "var {canon}") ; } None => { let _ = writeln ! (s , "free {x}") ; } } } Node :: Let { id , bound , body } => { let canon = format ! ("v{counter}") ; * counter += 1 ; let _ = writeln ! (s , "let {canon} =") ; write_canon (bound , depth + 1 , scope , counter , s) ; indent (depth , s) ; let _ = writeln ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A Core IR node."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Recursion-safety (RFC-0041 §4.5, W3):** [`Clone`], [`PartialEq`], and [`Drop`] are **manual,"] # [doc = " iterative** implementations (below the `impl Node` block), *not* `#[derive]`d — a derived"] # [doc = " (recursive) `Drop`/`Clone`/`PartialEq` overflows the native stack (`SIGABRT`, which violates the"] # [doc = " never-silent rule G2) on a deeply-nested node spine (`Let`/`App`/`Fix`/`Construct`/`Match`"] # [doc = " chains), even on the caller's ~2 MB stack outside any deep-stack worker. Only `Debug` stays"] # [doc = " derived. The content hash ([`Node::content_hash`] → `Canon::node`) is likewise iterative (see"] # [doc = " `content.rs`). This is a §6 within-freeze behavior-preserving hardening edit: no observable"] # [doc = " value/order change — clone/eq/hash are bit-identical to the derived forms (mutation-witnessed)."] # [derive (Debug)] pub enum Node { # [doc = " A constant value."] Const (Value) , # [doc = " A variable reference."] Var (VarId) , # [doc = " A let binding."] Let { # [doc = " Bound name."] id : VarId , # [doc = " The bound expression."] bound : Box < Node > , # [doc = " The body in which `id` is in scope."] body : Box < Node > , } , # [doc = " A paradigm-specific primitive application."] Op { # [doc = " The primitive."] prim : Prim , # [doc = " Operands."] args : Vec < Node > , } , # [doc = " The only node that changes a value's representation; always carries a policy (WF1/WF2)."] Swap { # [doc = " The value being converted."] src : Box < Node > , # [doc = " The target representation."] target : Repr , # [doc = " The policy that chose/justified the swap."] policy : PolicyRef , } , # [doc = " A saturated constructor application (RFC-0011 §4.1, r3): builds a data value. SC-3-transparent"] # [doc = " (Repr-transparent — no `Swap`). `args.len()` must equal the constructor's field count (WF6);"] # [doc = " saturation is *checked* against the data registry above the kernel."] Construct { # [doc = " The constructor (`#T#i`)."] ctor : CtorRef , # [doc = " The field expressions, in declaration order (saturated, WF6)."] args : Vec < Node > , } , # [doc = " A **flat** pattern match (RFC-0011 §4.1, r3): one scrutinee, single-level constructor/literal"] # [doc = " alternatives, at most one default. Coverage is *checked* (WF7), never assumed; the Maranget"] # [doc = " decision tree that lowers nested surface patterns to this flat form stays an untrusted"] # [doc = " artifact above the kernel (RFC-0011 §4.4)."] Match { # [doc = " The value being scrutinised."] scrutinee : Box < Node > , # [doc = " The alternatives, tried first-match, left-to-right."] alts : Vec < Alt > , # [doc = " The catch-all branch, taken when no alternative matches."] default : Option < Box < Node > > , } , # [doc = " A **lambda abstraction** (RFC-0001 r4; RFC-0007 §4.1 `Lam`): binds one `param` in `body`. A"] # [doc = " `Lam` node *is* a function value (a normal form). The v0 surface is first-order, so an"] # [doc = " elaborated `Lam` is **closed** (free only in `param` + global/`Fix` names) — no captured"] # [doc = " environment (RFC-0007 §4.7: recursion is through definitions, never heap closures). Multiple"] # [doc = " arguments are curried (`λx. λy. …`). The param *type* is checked above the kernel and is **not**"] # [doc = " an L0 node field (like `Let`, the post-typecheck core is untyped — identity is structural)."] Lam { # [doc = " The bound parameter."] param : VarId , # [doc = " The body, in scope of `param`."] body : Box < Node > , } , # [doc = " **Application** (RFC-0001 r4; RFC-0007 §4.1 `App`): apply `func` to `arg`, call-by-value. A"] # [doc = " saturated multi-arg call is a left-nested chain `App(App(f, a), b)`."] App { # [doc = " The function being applied (reduces to a `Lam`)."] func : Box < Node > , # [doc = " The argument."] arg : Box < Node > , } , # [doc = " **General recursion** (RFC-0001 r4; RFC-0007 §4.1 `Fix`; R7-Q1 — a node, not a recursive-`Let`"] # [doc = " flag). `Fix{name, body}` binds `name` to the whole `Fix` in `body` (self-reference), and"] # [doc = " unfolds by substitution — `Fix(f, e) ⟶ e[f ↦ Fix(f, e)]` — under the interpreter's fuel clock"] # [doc = " (so a non-productive recursion is an explicit budget exhaustion, never a hang; RFC-0007 §4.5,"] # [doc = " CakeML). Mutual-recursion `Fix` *groups* are deferred to a later step (R7-Q3); v0 elaborates"] # [doc = " only self-recursion."] Fix { # [doc = " The self-reference name bound in `body`."] name : VarId , # [doc = " The recursive body (typically a `Lam`)."] body : Box < Node > , } , # [doc = " **Mutual recursion** — a binding group (RFC-0001 r5; R7-Q3). `FixGroup{defs, body}` binds every"] # [doc = " `defs[i].0` to `defs[i].1` **simultaneously**: each definition *and* `body` see all the group's"] # [doc = " names, so two functions can call each other. It is the n-way generalisation of [`Node::Fix`]"] # [doc = " (the n=1 self-recursion case); the elaborator emits a `FixGroup` only for a strongly-connected"] # [doc = " call group of **≥2** functions and leaves direct self-recursion on `Fix` (so the simpler node's"] # [doc = " semantics and tests are untouched). Like `Fix`, it unfolds by substitution under the"] # [doc = " interpreter's fuel clock — never a hang. Reduction has two cases (mirroring `Fix`'s single"] # [doc = " unfold): a **focus** `FixGroup(defs, fᵢ)` unfolds to `defs[i][fⱼ ↦ FixGroup(defs, fⱼ)]` (the"] # [doc = " member's definition with the group re-bound), and a **continuation** `FixGroup(defs, e)` (with"] # [doc = " `e` not a bare member name) unfolds to `e[fⱼ ↦ FixGroup(defs, fⱼ)]`. The member names are"] # [doc = " `%`-fresh (no surface capture), and the group **binds** all of them — substitution shadows"] # [doc = " them, so the per-member focus thunks stay intact across the unfold."] FixGroup { # [doc = " The mutually-recursive bindings `(name, definition)`, each typically a `Lam`. Order is the"] # [doc = " elaborator's callee-stable order; identity is over the α-normalised group (content hash)."] defs : Vec < (VarId , Box < Node >) > , # [doc = " The continuation, in scope of every bound name in `defs`."] body : Box < Node > , } , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The content-addressed **prim table `Π`** (RFC-0007 §4.4; R7-Q4): resolved declarations keyed by"] # [doc = " their content hash, plus the build-time `name → hash` resolution used to form [`PrimRef`]s — the"] # [doc = " same two-map shape as [`DataRegistry`](crate::DataRegistry), so a prim's identity (`#p`) is the"] # [doc = " same on every path (the NFR-7 differential is over *one* prim set, never two)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Prims have no inter-references (unlike data, which can be mutually recursive), so building is a"] # [doc = " flat hash-and-insert — no SCC/cycle handling is needed."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Default , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct PrimTable { # [doc = " Resolved declarations, keyed by content hash."] decls : BTreeMap < ContentHash , PrimDecl > , # [doc = " Build-time (kernel) name → content hash (names are metadata — ADR-003)."] by_name : BTreeMap < String , ContentHash > , }

distinct from"] # [doc = " the pre-existing unsigned `bit.add`/`bit.sub`, which under-refuse relative to the signed"] # [doc = " domain), and the **signedness-split signed op set** (`bin.div_s`/`bin.rem_s`/`bin.shr_s` +"] # [doc = " `cmp.lt_s`, RFC-0033 §4.1.2/§4.1.3, M-767 — signed truncated division/remainder, the"] # [doc = " arithmetic right shift, and the two's-complement ordering, each a distinct named op from"] # [doc = " its unsigned counterpart per ADR-028), and the **dense elementwise group**"] # [doc = " (`dense.add`/`dense.sub`/`dense.neg`/`dense.scale`, RFC-0001 §4.1/RFC-0002 §5, M-890 —"] # [doc = " `enb` Gap C; [Trit :: Zero ; m] ; let mut carry = Trit :: Zero ; for i in (0 .. m) . rev () { let (d , c) = add_with_carry (a [i] , b [i] , carry) ; out [i] = d ; carry = c ; } if carry != Trit :: Zero { return None ; } Some (out) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Fixed-width multiplication. Computes the full product by shifted accumulation (independent of"] # [doc = " machine integer multiply) in a `2m`-trit buffer, then returns the low `m` trits iff the high"] # [doc = " trits are all zero — otherwise `None` (overflow, explicit)."] # [must_use] pub fn mul (a : & [Trit] , b : & [Trit]) -> Option < Vec < Trit > > { if a . len () != b . len () { return None ; } let m = a . len () ; if m == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let wide = 2 * m ; let mut acc = vec ! [Trit :: Zero ; wide] ; for (k , & bk) in b . iter () . rev () . enumerate () { let factor = digit (bk) ; if factor == 0 { continue ; } let a_signed : Vec < Trit > = if factor < 0 { neg (a) } else { a . to_vec () } ; let mut partial_lsb = vec ! [Trit :: Zero ; wide] ; for (j , & t) in a_signed . iter () . rev () . enumerate () { partial_lsb [k + j] = t ; } let mut acc_msb : Vec < Trit > = acc . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; let partial_msb : Vec < Trit > = partial_lsb . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; acc_msb = add (& acc_msb , & partial_msb) ? ; acc = acc_msb . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; } if acc [m ..] . iter () . any (| & t | t != Trit :: Zero) { return None ; } let low_msb : Vec < Trit > = acc [.. m] . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; Some (low_msb) }

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " **Exhaustive truth-table proof** of the shared balanced full-adder over all 27 inputs: the"] # [doc = " digit identity `a + b + carry_in == digit_out + 3·carry_out` holds exactly. This is the"] # [doc = " regression guard for the DRY extraction — both [`add`] and [`BigTernary`] ripple this one"] # [doc = " primitive, so a broken row fails here immediately (alongside `add_matches_integer_oracle`)."] # [test] fn add_with_carry_is_exhaustively_correct () { for a in [Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos] { for b in [Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos] { for c in [Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos] { let (d , carry) = add_with_carry (a , b , c) ; assert_eq ! (digit (a) + digit (b) + digit (c) , digit (d) + 3 * digit (carry) , "full-adder identity for ({a:?}, {b:?}, {c:?})") ; } } } } # [doc = " Walk every integer representable in `m` trits, paired with its codec encoding."] fn each_in_range (m : u32 , mut f : impl FnMut (i64 , Vec < Trit >)) { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for v in - max ..= max { f (v , int_to_trits (v , m) . expect ("in range")) ; } } # [test] fn worked_example_matches_spec () { let t = int_to_trits (- 78 , 6) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (t , vec ! [Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos , Trit :: Zero]) ; assert_eq ! (trits_to_int (& t) , - 78) ; } # [test] fn range_is_symmetric () { assert_eq ! (max_magnitude (1) , Some (1)) ; assert_eq ! (max_magnitude (6) , Some (364)) ; assert_eq ! (int_to_trits (365 , 6) , None) ; assert_eq ! (int_to_trits (- 365 , 6) , None) ; } # [test] fn codec_round_trips_exhaustively () { for m in 1 ..= 5 { each_in_range (m , | v , t | { assert_eq ! (t . len () , m as usize) ; assert_eq ! (trits_to_int (& t) , v , "round-trip at m={m}") ; }) ; } } # [test] fn neg_is_value_negation () { for m in 1 ..= 5 { each_in_range (m , | v , t | { assert_eq ! (trits_to_int (& neg (& t)) , - v , "neg at m={m}") ; }) ; } } # [doc = " **Oracle property test (add):** the digit-wise ripple-carry adder agrees with the `i64`"] # [doc = " oracle for *every* pair at small widths — in range it equals the encoded sum, out of range"] # [doc = " it is `None`."] # [test] fn add_matches_integer_oracle () { for m in 1 ..= 4 { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for x in - max ..= max { for y in - max ..= max { let a = int_to_trits (x , m) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (y , m) . unwrap () ; let got = add (& a , & b) ; let expected = x + y ; if expected . abs () <= max { assert_eq ! (got , int_to_trits (expected , m) , "add {x}+{y} at m={m}") ; } else { assert_eq ! (got , None , "add {x}+{y} should overflow at m={m}") ; } } } } } # [test] fn sub_matches_integer_oracle () { for m in 1 ..= 4 { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for x in - max ..= max { for y in - max ..= max { let a = int_to_trits (x , m) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (y , m) . unwrap () ; let got = sub (& a , & b) ; let expected = x - y ; if expected . abs () <= max { assert_eq ! (got , int_to_trits (expected , m) , "sub {x}-{y} at m={m}") ; } else { assert_eq ! (got , None , "sub {x}-{y} should overflow at m={m}") ; } } } } } # [doc = " **Oracle property test (mul):** the shifted-add multiplier agrees with the `i64` oracle for"] # [doc = " every pair at small widths."] # [test] fn mul_matches_integer_oracle () { for m in 1 ..= 4 { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for x in - max ..= max { for y in - max ..= max { let a = int_to_trits (x , m) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (y , m) . unwrap () ; let got = mul (& a , & b) ; let expected = x * y ; if expected . abs () <= max { assert_eq ! The returned NaN, if any, carries the canonical bits"] # [doc = " ([`CANONICAL_NAN_BITS`]) by the construction invariant of [`Value::new`]."] # [must_use] pub fn float (& self) -> Option < f64 > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Float (x) => Some (* x) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The element count of a [`Repr::Seq`] value, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — a non-sequence has no length here, G2). `Exact`: a total decidable query."] # [must_use] pub fn seq_len (& self) -> Option < usize > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Seq (elems) => Some (elems . len ()) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Never-silent indexed access into a [`Repr::Seq`] value (RFC-0032 D3): the `i`-th element, or"] # [doc = " `None` when `i` is out of bounds **or** the value is not a sequence — **never** a panic or a"] # [doc = " silent default (G2). `Exact`: total over its domain. The `.myc` `Vec::get` surface bottoms"] # [doc = " out on this."] # [must_use] pub fn seq_get (& self , i : usize) -> Option < & Value > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Seq (elems) => elems . get (i) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The elements of a [`Repr::Seq`] value as a slice, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (the fold/iterate basis — RFC-0032 D3). Never-silent: a non-sequence yields `None`, not an"] # [doc = " empty slice."] # [must_use] pub fn seq_elems (& self) -> Option < & [Value] > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Seq (elems) => Some (elems) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The byte length of a [`Repr::Bytes`] value, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — a non-bytes value has no byte length here). `Exact`. RFC-0032 D4."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes_len (& self) -> Option < usize > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) => Some (b . len ()) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Never-silent indexed byte access into a [`Repr::Bytes`] value (RFC-0032 D4): the `i`-th byte,"] # [doc = " or `None` when `i` is out of bounds **or** the value is not a byte string — **never** a panic"] # [doc = " or a silent default (G2). `Exact`: total over its domain."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes_get (& self , i : usize) -> Option < u8 > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) => b . get (i) . copied () , _ => None , } } # [doc = " Never-silent byte sub-slice `[start, end)` of a [`Repr::Bytes`] value (RFC-0032 D4): `None`"] # [doc = " when the range is out of bounds or inverted, or the value is not a byte string — **never** a"] # [doc = " panic or a silently-clamped range (G2). `Exact`."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes_slice (& self , start : usize , end : usize) -> Option < & [u8] > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) if start <= end && end <= b . len () => Some (& b [start .. end]) , _ => None , } } # [doc = " The bytes of a [`Repr::Bytes`] value as a slice, or `None` for any other representation"] # [doc = " (never-silent — not an empty slice). RFC-0032 D4."] # [must_use] pub fn bytes (& self) -> Option < & [u8] > { match self . payload () { Payload :: Bytes (b) => Some (b) , _ => None , } } }

gaps.snippet: fn payload_matches (repr : & Repr , payload : & Payload) -> bool { match (repr , payload) { (Repr :: Binary { width } , Payload :: Bits (b)) => b . len () == * width as usize , (Repr :: Ternary { trits } , Payload :: Trits (t)) => t . len () == * trits as usize , (Repr :: Dense { dim , .. } , Payload :: Scalars (s)) => s . len () == * dim as usize , (Repr :: Vsa { dim , .. } , Payload :: Hypervector (h)) => h . len () == * dim as usize , (Repr :: Float { .. } , Payload :: Float (_)) => true , (Repr :: Seq { elem , len } , Payload :: Seq (elems)) => { elems . len () == * len as usize && elems . iter () . all (| e | e . repr () == elem . as_ref ()) } (Repr :: Bytes , Payload :: Bytes (_)) => true , _ => false , } }

gaps.snippet: nodule core.wrapping;
gaps.reason: derived nodule path `core.wrapping` has segment(s) [wrapping] colliding with a Mycelium reserved word — emitting them verbatim would fail to parse (`nodule core.wrapping;` tokenizes the colliding word as a keyword, not an identifier); each colliding segment is escaped with the `_kw` suffix (now `core.wrapping_kw`) rather than dropped, so distinct source files whose only differing segment is a reserved word (e.g. `l1.fuse` vs `l1.nodule`) stay distinguishable instead of both collapsing onto the same nodule path (G2/VR-5)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The explicit, named Axis-B opt-out (RFC-0034 §10; M-791)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Present on a [`Meta`](crate::meta::Meta) only when the producing operation was given a"] # [doc = " **named, explicit** `wrapping` annotation at the use site. Its absence is the default state"] # [doc = " (never-silent Axis-B failability applies). Its presence marks \"the caller explicitly requested"] # [doc = " wraparound; this is visible and auditable.\""] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `WrappingOpt` does **not** alter the guarantee strength (`Exact`/`Proven`/…) or the"] # [doc = " certification mode (`Fast`/`Certified`/…); those are Axis-A and certification axes and remain"] # [doc = " orthogonal (VR-5). A `wrapping` annotation is \"I chose wraparound here, explicitly\" — it is"] # [doc = " not \"I lowered the accuracy bar\"."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , Copy , PartialEq , Eq)] pub struct WrappingOpt { # [doc = " Reason field — reserved for v1 (M-791): a developer-supplied justification string (like the"] # [doc = " `// SAFETY:` convention for `unsafe`). In v0 the only legal value is `()` (the unit token is"] # [doc = " the explicit acknowledgement: no magic string, no default comment). The field is private so"] # [doc = " the stable public surface is just `WrappingOpt::new()`."] _reason : () , }
